questions.question: List and briefly describe two key challenges associated with implementing IoT solutions in a smart city context.
keyPoints: Security and Privacy: Managing sensitive data from citizens and infrastructure
Interoperability: Ensuring various IoT devices from different vendors can communicate effectively

questions.question: Explain how an IoT-based smart traffic management system works and discuss one of its primary benefits.
keyPoints: Uses sensors, cameras, and GPS data to monitor real-time traffic flow
Data sent to cloud platform for AI-based analysis and pattern identification
System adjusts traffic signal timing automatically to optimize flow
Primary benefit: reduced commute times and decreased vehicle emissions

questions.question: Name the five core components of a smart city and explain the role of IoT in each.
keyPoints: Infrastructure: IoT sensors monitor structural health of bridges and roads
Transportation: traffic sensors and GPS optimize routing
Energy: smart meters and grid sensors optimize distribution
Waste: fill-level sensors in bins optimize collection routes
Public Safety: cameras and sensors enable crime detection and emergency response

questions.question: Critically evaluate the role of IoT in achieving urban sustainability, discussing both benefits and potential drawbacks.
keyPoints: Benefits: Smart grids integrate renewables, waste sensors reduce fuel use, environmental monitoring enables policy
Drawbacks: Manufacturing and disposal of billions of IoT devices creates e-waste
Digital divide may exclude disadvantaged populations from smart city benefits
Privacy concerns from city-wide surveillance and data collection
